content.problemStatement:
You are tasked with creating a program that defines a structure to store information about a student (name, roll number, and marks). The program should then pass an instance of this structure to a function, which will display the student's information.


Input Format
The input will consist of three lines:



Student's name (string, maximum 50 characters).
Student's roll number (integer).
Student's marks (float).


Output Format
The output should display the student's information in the following format.

Name: [Student's name]

Roll Number: [Student's roll number]

Marks: [Student's marks]
content.inputFormat: First line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. For each test case, three lines follow: the student's name (a string of up to 50 characters, may contain spaces), the roll number (integer), and the marks (floating‑point number).
content.outputFormat:
For each test case output exactly three lines in the following format:
Name: [student's name]
Roll Number: [roll number]
Marks: [marks printed with two decimal places]

sampleTestCases.explanation: One test case. The name is Alice, roll number 123, marks 95.5 which is printed with two decimal places.
One test case. Marks 78.9 become 78.90 after formatting.

sampleTestCases.output:
Name: John Doe
Roll Number: 7
Marks: 88.00
sampleTestCases.explanation: Name contains a space. Marks 88 are printed as 88.00.

hidden.label: uniform/edge case
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name: Bob
Roll Number: 5
Marks: 50.50
Name: Bob
Roll Number: 5
Marks: 50.50
Name: Bob
Roll Number: 5
Marks: 50.50

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Name: Alice
Roll Number: 123
Marks: 95.50
Name: Bob Smith
Roll Number: 456
Marks: 78.90
Name: Charlie
Roll Number: 1
Marks: 0.00
Name: Delta
Roll Number: 1000
Marks: 100.00

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases. For each test case read the name using a full line read (to keep spaces), then read the roll number and marks. Print the data exactly as required, formatting the marks with two decimal places using standard formatting facilities. The algorithm is O(T) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, where T is the number of test cases

code.Java:
import java.io.*;import java.util.*;public class Main{public static void main(String[] args) throws Exception{BufferedReader br=new BufferedReader(new InputStreamReader(System.in));String line=br.readLine();if(line==null||line.isEmpty())return;int T=Integer.parseInt(line.trim());StringBuilder sb=new StringBuilder();for(int t=0;t<T;t++){String name=br.readLine();if(name==null)name="";String rollLine=br.readLine();String marksLine=br.readLine();int roll=Integer.parseInt(rollLine.trim());double marks=Double.parseDouble(marksLine.trim());sb.append("Name: ").append(name).append('
');sb.append("Roll Number: ").append(roll).append('
');sb.append(String.format(java.util.Locale.US,"Marks: %.2f",marks));if(t!=T-1)sb.append('
');}System.out.print(sb.toString());}}
